architecturalFeatures.en: Small official temple layout retaining the clean, dignified characteristics of Qing official temple architecture — minimal ornamentation conveying the solemnity of state ritual.
architecturalFeatures.zh: 規模較小的官廟格局，保留清代官式廟宇簡潔莊重的建築特徵，不過度裝飾，體現官方祭祀的肅穆氣氛。

en: Note: Taiwan's only officially built Qing-era Wind God temple
zh: 特色：台灣唯一清代官建風神廟

historicalContext.en: Established during the Qianlong era (18th century) as an official site for prayers before navigating the Luermen waterway, reflecting Qing-dynasty concern for harbor trade safety.
historicalContext.zh: 乾隆年間（18世紀）設立，原為船隻進出鹿耳門水道前的官方祈安場所，反映清代對港口貿易安全的重視。

modernUse.en: Now a city-designated historic site maintaining daily worship functions; serves as an important interpretive site for Tainan's harbor cultural history.
modernUse.zh: 現為市定古蹟，維持日常祭祀功能，並作為台南港口文化史的重要詮釋場域。

description.en: Temple facade: Shoot straight-on with symmetric framing to convey the dignified simplicity of official temple architecture.
description.zh: 廟宇立面：正面平視拍攝，以對稱構圖呈現官廟莊重簡樸的立面特徵。

description.en: View toward Luermen: Shoot from the temple entrance toward the harbor direction to evoke the historical sense of prayers before departure.
description.zh: 鹿耳門方向遠眺：站在廟前向港口方向拍攝，感受古代船隻出港前祈安的歷史空間感。

description.en: Plaque close-up: Shoot upward at interior plaques; focus on Qing-era calligraphy style and the contrast of gold characters.
description.zh: 匾額特寫：仰角拍攝廟內匾額題字，注意清代書法風格與金字對比。

whyItMatters.en: An official Qing-dynasty site linking religious ritual with port administration. One of the few surviving Wind God temples in Taiwan, symbolizing systematic official management of harbor activity.
whyItMatters.zh: 清代官方祭祀風神之所，反映台南信仰與港口行政治理緊密結合的歷史。此廟是清代官方系統性管理港口的具體象徵，台灣現存少數以風神為主祀的廟宇。
